applicability_boundaries: N=20: Expert-prior-dominated CPTs, no meaningful data learning
Diagnostic replay checks internal consistency, NOT predictive validation
Site-specific marginals informative; CPTs essentially identical to general model

decision_records.title: Site Model Framing
decision_records.summary: Site-specific BN-RRM for Toquaht Bay Marina and Campground is framed as a demonstration/case study model. With N=20 stations, CPTs are >80% expert-prior-dominated. No validation or accuracy claims are supported at this sample size.

decision_records.title: Hierarchical Borrowing from General Model
decision_records.summary: Site-specific CPTs fitted using hierarchical borrowing: general model CPTs serve as informative priors in BDeu estimation. Tier 1 (Noisy-OR) borrowed wholesale. Tiers 2-4 use alpha values {'tier2': 3.0, 'tier3': 5.0, 'tier4': 4.0} controlling general-vs-site data blend. Parent pack: bnrrm-general-v1.0-dev-map.

known_limitations.title: Expert-prior dominance (N=20)
known_limitations.description: With only 20 training stations, the BDeu data contribution is negligible. CPTs are >80% determined by expert priors. The site model's primary value is in site-specific marginal distributions, not data-learned conditional probabilities.

known_limitations.title: No independent validation labels
known_limitations.description: ALCAN has no consultant-assessed WOE labels in the database. 'Observed risk' is derived from the model's own heuristic (_determine_observed_risk), creating circularity in any LOO-style evaluation.
